name: Taco Soup
directions: In a large saucepan (I use a Dutch-oven), cook beef until no longer pink; drain.
Stir in taco seasoning, cumin, red pepper, corn, beans and tomatoes.
Cover and simmer for at least 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Place tortilla chips in soup bowls; ladle soup over chips.
Sprinkle with cheese.

name: Chunky Taco Soup
directions: In large pot brown beef and onion in oil.
Add the pinto beans, tomatoes, water, black beans, creamstyle corn, salad dressing mix and taco seasoning.
Bring to a boil.
Reduce heat; cover and simmer for 20- 30 minutes or until meat is tender.
Stiring in between this time every 5 mimutes.

similar.name: Taco Soup With Beans and Baked Tortillas

name: Taco Soup (Paula Deen)
directions: Brown the ground beef and onions in a large skillet; drain off excess fat and transfer browned beef and onions to a large slow cooker or stockpot.

Add the beans, corn, tomatoes (I like to mash all of the tomato products before I put them in the stockpot--sometimes there are very large chunks of tomatoes, especially in the stewed tomatoes), green chilies, black olives, green olives, taco seasoning and ranch dressing mix.
Simmer over low heat for about 1 hour (or 6-8 hours on low setting in crock pot).
To serve, place a few corn chips in each bowl and ladle soup over them.
Top with sour cream, cheese, green onions and jalapeños.

similar.name: Taco Soup With Beans and Baked Tortillas

name: Taco Soup With Beans and Baked Tortillas
directions: In a large pot brown the beef with onions, garlic, jalapeno peppers and chili flakes (if using) until well browned (about 12-15 minutes; drain and return to pot.
Add in all the remaining ingredients except the tortillas; mix to combine.
Cover and simmer for about 1-1/2 hours (simmer uncovered the last 30 minutes of cooking).
Season with black pepper and salt if desired.
Thin out with more water or tomato juice if necessary.
Place the tortilla strips on a greased baking sheet, sprinkle with salt if desired and bake in a 375 degree oven for about 8 minutes.
Ladle the soup into bowl and top with sour cream, grated cheddar cheese and baked tortilla strips.

name: Weight Watchers Taco Soup
directions: Brown meat and onion, drain and add remaining ingredients.  Bring to a boil then reduce heat and simmer 30 minutes.  2 points per cup!

name: Taco Soup
directions: Cook beef and onion until cooked all the way through (meat will not be pink anymore).
Drain.
Add remaining ingredients.
simmer for 1 hour.
enjoy!

similar.name: Taco Soup With Beans and Baked Tortillas

name: Taco Soup
directions: Brown meat and onion in large skillet; then drain.
Combine all remaining ingredients with the ground beef in a large pot.
Simmer uncovered for 45 minutes, or until beans are tender and soup has thickened, stirring occasionally.

similar.name: Taco Soup With Beans and Baked Tortillas

name: Ww Taco Soup
directions: Brown ground beef, drain fat.
Add onion, cook 2 minutes.
Add both dry mixes and 2 cups of water.  Stir well.
Add remaining ingredients.
Cook 5-10 minutes.  2 WW points per serving.

name: Taco Soup
directions: Brown ground beef with onion, drain.
Combine all ingredients except cheese and fritos in a 6 quart pan.
Bring to a simmer and cook for 30 to 40 minutes.
Spoon into bowls and top with cheddar cheese and fritos!

name: Taco Soup
directions: Brown beef.
Add rest of ingredience.
bring to boil and simmer.
top with cheese.
may add sour cream.

similar.name: Taco Soup With Beans and Baked Tortillas

name: Beef and Bean Taco Soup
directions: Dice onion, carrot and celery.  Drain and rinse beans.
In large soup pot add oil and butter, after melting add onion, carrot and celery; season with salt (teaspoon, approx).  Let sweat 5 mins; lid on.
Add ground sirloin, break into small pieces and brown.  Season with salt, pepper and garlic.
When beef is brown, add corn, green chilies,beans, diced tomatoes, tomato sauce, paprika, bay leaves and taco seasoning.  Stir well.  Bring to a boil on medium heat; lid on. Simmer 5 minutes.
Add beef broth.  Cover and bring to a simmer, 10 minutes.
Soup can cook longer, if preferred - but is delicious at the 10 minute mark.
